entry.title: shipment approval process for sales orders 銷售訂單出貨審核
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

I would like to ask if there is a shipment approval process for sales orders. To reduce discrepancies between picking quantities and order quantities, we hope to add a shipment review step.

When shipping, we use the "Create Outbound Deliveries" and "Pick Outbound Delivery" apps. Is it possible to add a shipment review process between these steps?

Thank you!

entry.title: Since creating Return to Supplier is not supported for kit, is there any workaround?
entry.content:
According to KBA '2931205 - Error " Item 1: Kit Product is not allowed" Appears While Creating Return to Supplier for kit Items', it is not supported to creating Return to Supplier for kit, then is there any workaround?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Since creating Return to Supplier is not supported fo...
entry.content: Workaround: reverse original inbound delivery document flow according to KBA '1347361 - How to Reverse / Cancel Inbound Delivery'.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - Semantic Onboarding - Dimensions / Text View - Usability
entry.content:
Dear Community,
I appreciate the new capability to import semantically-rich objects from your SAP systems. I have a few questions about issues that I noticed while dealing with this.

(1) Technical Name
The picture below shows the result after a successful upload with the option: "Replication Flows to Local Table". In result the automatic naming convention is Remote.{Connection}.{CDS View}. This is a bit irritating because a locale table is created. In case I'm onboarding an view with the option: "Remote Table" the same naming convention is used. 

Well this leads me to fact that the mechanism of Semantic Onboarding does not separate between source type like Remote.{Connection}.{CDS View} or Local.{Connection}.{CDS View}. Additionally, I don't seem to be able to import a view for federation and replication.

What is your recommendation to achieve both integration scenarios via Semantic Onboarding? (Of course, there is the known capability of "Import Remote Table").

(2) Semantic Usage
The picture below shows the result after a successful upload with the option: "Replication Flows to Local Table". The field Semantic Usage shall me indicate how can i use this artefact for. In that case it is of type dimension. 

The picture below shows the result after a successful upload with the option: "Replication Flows to Local Table". As result of semantic onboarding all semantic types were taken over. But the last column is very import for a decent dimension artefact. The different semantic types or associations are made usable for further usage. But this column is empty and loads of warnings appear to this issue.

If I worked with this automatic result, I always had to create an additional dimension view on top. Well, that is certainly relevant for further modeling requirements. But what is your recommendation to use this "Ready to Go" - Feature (Semantic Onboarding)?

Thanks
Nico

entry.title: Re: Account determination for interests posting in public...
entry.content:
Hello,
Thank you for your question.
After talking to the experts, I can provide the following answer: The feature you mentioned is neither available nor planned to be available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
You may request features on the Customer Influence Portal. We continuously review and prioritize feature requests based on customer feedback.
Kind regards,
Anne

entry.title: Re: Question About Associating Attributes Across Dimensio...
entry.content:
@Bernardo_Santos1 

That looks like a basic way to go for your model. 

Go for it.

entry.title: Re: SAP EPPM and SAP PS
entry.content:
Hi akash

In EPPM entity "Initiative" corresponds to a program. Using Initiatives you can (bi-directional) sync gates, status and schedules of a global program with multiple subprojects.

For loosely coupled projects you can also use the concept of "Collections"  in EPPM.

Best regards Renato

entry.title: Re: Import external service odata v4 , Error : No metadat...
entry.content:
Thanks Dinu it's good now 🙂

it's weird because it's working with command "npm exec cds import ..." but not with command "cds import ..."

entry.title: Error: Error during request to remote service: Failed to build headers.
entry.content:
Hello, community!

I am encountering an issue while working with an SAP CAP-based application. Despite my best efforts to debug, I haven’t been able to resolve it. I'm sharing the details here, hoping someone can guide me toward a solution.

Error Details

The error message I’m facing is as follows:


Context

I’m working on a CAP application where I need to integrate with an external OData v2 service hosted on SAP SuccessFactors (SFSF). The connection is configured to use an SAP BTP destination. I’m relying on the @SAP/cds framework to handle the OData service requests.

Package.json Configuration

Here's the relevant portion of my package.json:

 

Connection Handler Code

This is the function I’m using to connect to the backend:

Custom Handler

Service Implementation:

Service.cds

  

Service Handler:

 

Troubleshooting Steps Tried

  1. Verified all required dependencies and their versions.

  2. Configured the destination correctly in SAP BTP with valid URL, username, and password.

  3. Added forwardAuthToken: true to package.json under the credentials for SFSF.

  4. Ensured the model path is correctly mapped.

  5. Checked network connectivity and ensured no firewalls are blocking requests.

 

 

 

Question to the Community

Despite implementing these steps, I continue to encounter the error "Error: Error during request to remote service: Failed to build headers." Could someone with experience in CAP-SuccessFactors integration share insights or steps I might have missed?

 

If you've faced and resolved a similar issue or have expertise in this area, I would be grateful for your guidance. Please share your thoughts in the comments below.

Thank you in advance for your support!

SAP Community, SAP TechEd  SAP BTP cockpit SAP Cloud Application Programming Model , Node.js API SAP SuccessFactors Learning

entry.title: Mass Processing in Resource Related Billing for Service with Advanced Execution Item
entry.content:
Mass Processing in Resource Related Billing for Service with Advanced Execution Item 

This blog will help you to understand how mass processing is done in the Resource Related Billing for Service. 

DP92 transaction is used as Resource Related Billing (RRB) solution for Service Order with Advanced Execution Items. DP92 will take Actual Costs from the Plant Maintenance Order (PMO) into account and creates new Debit Memo Request (DMR) and / or Credit Memo Request (CMR) types. The account assignment object used on the service side is New Fin Item-Based accounting.  

Customer Service (CS) module supports massive generation of Debit Memo Requests (DMR) and / or Credit Memo Requests (CMR) via DP95 transaction Also earlier versions of RRB solution provide capability to process one Service Order Item at once 

 To overcome the limitation of processing single Service Order Item and to provide feature parity for existing CS customers a new feature is being enabled for processing multiple Service Orders with capability to generate one or many billing document requests for each of the service orders. The mass processing in resource related billing for advanced execution item has been introduced. 

New Transaction Code ‘DP92A’ has been created, along with that newly created report  "RVPKMASS_MCSV" will be called upon running DP92A transaction, this report will call the FM ‘DIPCOL_COLLECTIVE_MCSV_RRB’ to process the selected service order items.  

Selection Screen:  

Significance of each input field: 

Service Order 

Enter the Service Order Ex. 8000016785 

Service Order Item 

Enter the Service Order Item Ex. 00010 

Sales Organization 

Enter the Sales Organization Ex.1710 

Distribution Channel 

Enter the Distribution Channel Ex.10 

Division 

Enter the Division Ex.00 

Sales Office 

Enter sales office that is responsible for specific sales and distribution products and services.  Ex. O 50000231  

Sales Group 

Enter the Sales Group which is sales employees, responsible for processing specific products or services. Ex. SGrp001 

Created By 

Name of user who created the transaction. 

Posting Date 

The posting date contains the date on which the transaction took place. This date differs from the date of entry if transactions are not entered directly, but for example, entered in the system retroactively. 

Sold-To Party 

Enter the Sold-To-Party that is used in the Service Order Ex.17100001 

Bill-To Party 

Enter the Bill-To-Party that is used in the service order Ex. 17100002 

Payer 

Enter the Payer that is used in the service order Ex. CSM1000 

 
Cost Period Option: 

From Period 

Posting period when processing should begin and carried out. 

Fiscal Year From 

Fiscal year, in which processing should begin and be carried out. 

Posting Date To 

Date on which the  document was entered in financial accounting or cost accounting. 

Further Options: 

Use Saved Data 

This flag specifies whether data extract saved to be used or not. 

If the saved data is ‘X’ , In this case if no data is found from the extract, then it creates document/data extract from the search criteria provided  

Refresh  

Specifies whether the saved data for the object to be billed should be refreshed. If Saved data is ‘X’ and Refresh is ‘X’ then the system first reads the saved data and subsequently rereads the sources. 

Create Options: 

Save DMR/CMR Extract  

When Save DMR/CMR Extract is ‘X’. When selected report reads the selection criteria and process the same, Processed DIP line items are created as ‘saved data extract’.  

Save DMR/DMR Document 

When Save DMR/DMR Document is ’X’. Report reads the data from the search criteria provided by the user and generates sales documents.  

Processing Options: 

Test Run 

Processing type for programs which change data: 

  • When the flag is active: the program is executed normally, all checks in the program are performed, their results are output in various forms (as single message, as a collection of messages, or as a log), but existing data are not changed. 
  • When the flag is inactive: the program is executed normally. All data changes (change, delete, insert new data records) are made in the database (real run). 

Background Processing 

Specifies background processing 

Detail List 

Indicator “Generated detailed list”  

Parallel Processing 

Indicator “Parallel Processing” 

How to execute the Mass Processing for advanced execution order Item in RRB solution 

  • Enter the transaction DP92A in command prompt. 
  • Users will get below Input screen. Enter the set of service orders to create the DMR/CMR. 
  • Select “Save DMR/CMR Document” 
  • Test Run is Inactive. 
  • Select the Detail List. 
  • Click on execute. 

  • When the selected items processed successfully, report output will be displayed as below.  

In the output screen user can see the “Processed” and “Incorrect “category.

Select the processed category and click on detail list. User can view newly created DMR for each entered service order. 

If any error occurs while processing the selected items, report output will be displayed as below.   

Select the “Incorrect Category” and click on detail list to see the error message. User can see the error message for the item for which DMR/CMR not created. 

This is how user can successfully create the mass processing for DMR/CMR for advanced execution order items.

entry.title: 10 Bonnes habitudes à adopter pour devenir un #expert 🚀cette année sur SAP ? 💪🍎
entry.content:
Devenir un expert SAP ne se fait pas du jour au lendemain, mais en adoptant des habitudes clés, vous pouvez considérablement accélérer votre progression. Voici 10 habitudes à adopter pour devenir un expert SAP cette année :

1. Formation continue et approfondie :

Ne vous limitez pas aux formations de base. Explorez les formations avancées, les certifications spécifiques à un module (FI, MM, SD, PP, etc.) et les nouvelles technologies SAP (S/4HANA, SAP BTP, etc.).
Utilisez les ressources disponibles : SAP Learning Hub, openSAP (cours en ligne gratuits), SAP Community Network (blogs, forums), livres et tutoriels.
Restez à jour avec les dernières versions et innovations SAP. Suivez les annonces de SAP, participez à des webinaires et consultez les notes de version.

2. Expérience pratique et projets concrets :

Recherchez des opportunités de travailler sur des projets SAP réels. Même des missions courtes ou des projets internes peuvent être très enrichissants.
Créez votre propre environnement de test SAP. Cela vous permettra d'expérimenter, de tester des configurations et de résoudre des problèmes en toute sécurité.
Participez à des hackathons ou des compétitions SAP. C'est un excellent moyen de mettre vos compétences à l'épreuve et d'apprendre des autres.

3. Spécialisation et expertise ciblée :

Identifiez un domaine fonctionnel ou technique qui vous passionne et concentrez-vous dessus. Devenir un expert dans un domaine spécifique est plus valorisant que d'avoir une connaissance superficielle de plusieurs domaines.
Approfondissez vos connaissances sur les processus métiers liés à votre domaine d'expertise. Comprendre les enjeux métiers est crucial pour proposer des solutions SAP pertinentes.

4. Veille technologique et curiosité constante :

Suivez les blogs, les forums et les influenceurs SAP. Restez informé des dernières tendances, des bonnes pratiques et des solutions innovantes.
Lisez des études de cas et des témoignages clients. Cela vous donnera une perspective concrète sur l'utilisation de SAP dans différents contextes.
N'ayez pas peur d'explorer de nouvelles fonctionnalités et technologies SAP. La curiosité est un moteur d'apprentissage puissant.

5. Réseau et partage de connaissances :

Rejoignez des groupes d'utilisateurs SAP, des communautés en ligne et des événements (SAP Inside Track, SAP TechEd). Échangez avec d'autres experts, posez des questions et partagez votre expérience.
Contribuez à la communauté SAP en rédigeant des articles de blog, en répondant à des questions sur les forums ou en participant à des projets open source. Le partage de connaissances est une excellente façon d'apprendre et de se faire connaître.

 

6. Maîtrise de la documentation SAP :

Apprenez à naviguer efficacement dans la documentation SAP (SAP Help Portal). C'est une ressource inestimable pour trouver des informations précises et détaillées.
Utilisez les notes SAP (SAP Notes) pour résoudre les problèmes et comprendre les corrections apportées par SAP.

7. Développement des compétences en résolution de problèmes :

Entraînez-vous à résoudre des problèmes complexes en utilisant les outils de débogage et de diagnostic SAP.
Analysez les erreurs et les messages d'erreur pour comprendre les causes profondes des problèmes.
Développez une approche méthodique et rigoureuse pour la résolution de problèmes.

8. Communication et compétences interpersonnelles :

Améliorez vos compétences en communication écrite et orale. Être capable d'expliquer des concepts techniques complexes de manière claire et concise est essentiel.
Développez vos compétences en travail d'équipe et en gestion de projet. Les projets SAP impliquent souvent de travailler avec des équipes multidisciplinaires.

9. Organisation et gestion du temps :

Définissez des objectifs d'apprentissage clairs et réalistes.
Planifiez votre temps de manière efficace pour consacrer du temps à la formation, à la pratique et à la veille technologique.
Soyez persévérant et ne vous découragez pas face aux difficultés.

10. Passion et motivation :

Cultivez votre passion pour SAP et pour les technologies de l'information.
Restez motivé en vous fixant des défis et en célébrant vos succès.
Considérez l'apprentissage de #SAP comme un investissement à long terme dans votre carrière.

En adoptant ces 10 habitudes, vous vous donnez les moyens de progresser rapidement et de devenir un véritable expert SAP. N'oubliez pas que la clé du succès réside dans la constance, la curiosité et la passion.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Calender Task "partially successful"

Kind regards,

Ismail

entry.title: Re: How to use SAP Content Agent Service (Video)

Don't find the link to the video. Is it still available?

Best regards,

Sumit

entry.title: Here's a glossary of key logistics areas in SAP S/4HANA, covering the core components and so
entry.content:
I. Core Logistics Modules:

Materials Management (MM):
Procurement: Managing the purchasing of materials and services, including purchase requisitions, purchase orders, goods receipts, and invoice verification.
Inventory Management: Controlling the storage and movement of materials, including goods receipts, goods issues, stock transfers, and physical inventory.
Material Valuation: Determining the value of materials for accounting purposes.
Sales and Distribution (SD):
Sales Order Management: Processing customer orders, including order entry, pricing, availability checks, and order confirmation.
Shipping: Managing the delivery of goods to customers, including picking, packing, and transportation.
Billing: Creating invoices for delivered goods and services.
Transportation: Planning and executing the transportation of goods. (Often overlaps with TM)
Production Planning (PP):
Demand Management: Forecasting demand for products.
Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Planning the procurement and production of materials to meet demand.
Production Execution: Managing the production process, including shop floor control, production orders, and material movements.

II. Advanced Logistics Areas and Functionalities:

Extended Warehouse Management (EWM):
Advanced warehouse management capabilities, including inbound processing, putaway, picking, packing, shipping, and yard management.
Provides more granular control over warehouse operations than standard Inventory Management.
Transportation Management (TM):
Planning and executing transportation activities, including transportation planning, carrier selection, freight costing, and shipment tracking.
Provides more comprehensive transportation capabilities than standard SD Transportation.
Logistics Execution (LE):
Encompasses shipping, transportation, and warehouse management processes. Often used as a general term for the execution aspects of logistics.
Warehouse Management (WM) (Mostly Replaced by EWM):
Basic warehouse management functionalities within the core MM module. Many companies are migrating from WM to EWM for advanced features.
Advanced Planning and Scheduling (APS):
Enables advanced planning for production and supply chain, considering constraints and optimizing resource utilization. Often part of PP/DS (Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling).
Supply Chain Planning (SCP):
Encompasses a broader range of planning activities, including demand planning, supply planning, and network optimization.
Global Trade Services (GTS):
Manages international trade processes, including customs compliance, import/export regulations, and trade agreements.
Event Management:
Monitoring and managing events throughout the supply chain, such as delays, disruptions, and exceptions.

 

Track and Trace:
Tracking the movement of goods throughout the supply chain, providing visibility and traceability.

III. Key Concepts and Integrations:

Integration with Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO): Logistics processes generate financial transactions that are posted to FI and CO.
Integration with Quality Management (QM): Quality inspections are often integrated with logistics processes, such as goods receipt and production.
Integration with Plant Maintenance (PM): Maintenance activities can impact logistics processes, such as production and transportation.
Supply Chain Visibility: Providing real-time visibility into the status of goods and processes throughout the supply chain.
Supply Chain Optimization: Using advanced algorithms and techniques to optimize logistics processes, such as transportation routing and inventory levels.

Relationship between WM and EWM:

It's crucial to understand the relationship between WM and EWM. WM is the "classic" warehouse management functionality within core MM. EWM is a separate, more advanced application that offers significantly more features. In S/4HANA, SAP is pushing for EWM adoption for companies with complex warehousing needs. Basic warehousing can still be handled with Inventory Management in MM, but WM is being phased out in newer implementations.

This glossary provides a solid foundation for understanding logistics in SAP S/4HANA. The complexity and specific functionalities used will depend on the business requirements of the organization.

entry.title: How can i able to add the LDAP user using BI REST API's through java code.
entry.content:
Hi, recently i had a requirement in my application where i need to provide the LDAP access for the users using java code.

can please help me if there is a way to add the user with Ldap alias by using SAP REST API's through java code.

entry.title: Re: Error While running F.01 "please input Valid company...
entry.content:
Hi All, 

Resolved the issue.

We have a custom enhancement in T code F.01 to validate the user with Comp Code and Profit center. Thats why the message is. 

Regards,

Venu M

entry.title: SAP MDG: For Change CR Type - while changing any field data Error Message to be perform
entry.content:
Hi

For Change CR Type - soon after clicking on edit on Change Request & if any of the field getting change, error message should get display.

How to perform this for above, Like how to get the changed fields data before submit of CR ? 
From my side tried several methods to get, but nothing worked.

Thanks & Regards, 
Sandeep Suggu.
(SAP MDG Consultant)

SAP Master Data Governance SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: SAP EPPM and SAP PS
entry.content: Hi Akash, In EPPM, the "Initiative" entity is equivalent to a program. It allows you to sync gates, statuses, and schedules in both directions between a global program and its related subprojects. For more loosely connected projects, the "Collections" feature in EPPM can be used as an alternative approach. Best regards, Renato

entry.title: Re: What address to choose in SAP Learning Hub subscripti...
entry.content:
Hi @tg123 

I am not quite sure why you have not been able to purchase the SAP Learning Hub Standard edition license as an individual.
Please note that as an individual purchaser (B2I), customer can only make purchases by accepting SAP B2B Terms & Conditions.
As an individual purchaser, the customer should book the license under an organization (using their name as the company name and their home address as the postal address). For VAT purposes, the customer can use a dummy VAT registration number in their country's specific format.
Please follow the link provided here and fill out the information as mentioned above to proceed with placing the order.
If this does not solve the issue please let me know.
In addition, I assume you created a ticket. Would you mind to share the ticket number? 

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Sharepoint CSV-File integration in Datasphere's Data...
entry.content:
Hi @janpeteraxe1 and a Happy New Year! 🙂

Thanks for your hint, but unfortunately we already ran into this issue and changed the folder from "Shared Documents" to "Geteilte Dokumente". With this change, we achieved to see our folder structure in Sharepoint, but then we have the challenge described above, that we get an error while choosing the CSV-file.

BG,

Marco

entry.title: IC invoice idocs are accidental re-sent in VF02 after language code change of customer
entry.content:
Happy new year

We have a problem with Inter company invoices being re-sent via IDOC when a user go into VF02, to to the outputs screen and press save.

The problem is that the output is redetermined. I have found at least 2 OSS notes about this problem (for example 3431818) and all solutions says the same. 

"...field Multiple issuing should be removed from V/40."

This flag (T685B-MEHRF) has never been set, so this is not the root cause, but the problem is related. In my opinion the problem is that the SAP standard programming allows multiple outputs to the same partner even if the flag is not set. This can happen if language code of the output partner is changed in master data.

However I can't find this problem described in any OSS note or community thread.
Has any one else experienced this problem and found a solution?

Regards Thomas

entry.title: Unable to get table name for Tables or Table histories > 1 billion rows from SAP Focused Run alerts
entry.content:
As part of SAP Focused Run alerts, we could see alerts for "Tables or Table histories > 1 billion rows" but unable to get what is that table name where records or more than 1 billion rows.
From the alert graph we can see how many tables are more than 1 billion like (2 or 3etc).

How we can get exact table name where rows are more than 1 billion from SAP Focused RUN ?

entry.title: Perform Percentage Calculations in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Objective

To perform and capture different percentage metrics in SAP Datasphere to calculate different types of percentages as per the business use cases and display various percentage values with the symbol ‘%’ as a suffix. As it is known in BEx query designer there are pre-built percentage functions that are used to calculate different types of percentages as per the business requirement of the report. These functions are used in calculated key figures/formulas of a query such as below -

  1. %GT - Percentage share of Grand total (Overall Result)
  2. %CT - Percentage share of Cumulative Total (Values in a Result)

This article is completely based on my learning experience for the above two such similar functions in SAP Datasphere. I hope, this illustration and technical approach will be useful for those who are looking to perform percentage calculations based on the reporting and analytics use cases.

Approach

  1. Option to calculate using a Data Flow script node using Python and persist in a table
  2. Option to calculate using Windows functions in a SQL based view
  3. Option to calculate using Graphical View  

Illustration

  1. Option to calculate using a Data Flow script node using Python and persist in a table

 

%GT - Percentage Total of Grand total

Specifies how high the percentage share of the grand total. Grand total here means the result of the aggregation at the highest level of the list.

Code Snippet using Python:

def transform(df):

    df['ORDERQUANTITY'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'].astype('float64')

    df['TOTAL'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'].sum()

    df['PERCENT'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'] / df['TOTAL']

    return df

%CT – Percentage share of a values of a result

Specifies how high the percentage amount is regarding the result. Result here means the result of the aggregation at the next-highest level (intermediate result).

 

Code Snippet using Python

def transform(df):

    df['ORDERQUANTITY'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'].astype('float64')

    df['TOTAL'] = df.groupby('SALESDOCUMENT')['ORDERQUANTITY'].transform('sum')

    df['PERCENT'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'] / df['TOTAL']

    return df

Observations:

  1. Both the calculations in same node but different data frames for consolidation. Just for illustration purposes, the above code snippet is split into two data frames.
  2. Alternatively calculate the percentages in the following layers or the Analytical Model
  3. This option could be considered in the scenarios of fixed reporting and absence of dynamics with the dimensions2. Option to calculate using Windows functions in a SQL based view

Sample Dataset

Code Snippet using SQL view creation for calculating percentages:

select SALESDOCUMENT, SALESDOCUMENTITEM, sum(ORDERQUANTITY) as ORDERQUANTITY,

sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over ()  as GRAND_TOT_AMT,

sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over (ORDER BY SALESDOCUMENT, SALESDOCUMENTITEM)  as CUM_TOT_AMT,

sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over (PARTITION BY SALESDOCUMENT)  as ORDR_TOT_AMT,

CAST ( ( SUM(ORDERQUANTITY) / sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over () ) AS DECIMAL(10,2)) as GRAND_TOT_PERCENT,

 CAST ( ( SUM(ORDERQUANTITY) / sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over (PARTITION BY SALESDOCUMENT) ) AS DECIMAL(10,2)) as CUM_TOT_PERCENT

  from( 

       select SALESDOCUMENT, SALESDOCUMENTITEM, ORDERQUANTITY from "TST_VW_PERCENTAGE_CHCK" ----Change the view or table name and accordingly the fields

        )

        group by SALESDOCUMENT, SALESDOCUMENTITEM, ORDERQUANTITY

Observations:

  1. Alternatively calculate the percentages in the following layers or the Analytical Model
  2. This option could be considered in the scenarios of reporting primarily inclusive of dynamic filtering with the dimensions without any persistence3. Option to calculate totals and percentages using Graphical views

Code Snippet

  • Design the Graphical view calling thrice with inner join…while first node brings in at Sales Document Level which is the first, second node calculates the Grand total while there is no dimension that should be part of the result set, third node calculates the cumulative total based of the first level highest for example here is the sales document level.

 

  • Perform cumulative and Grand percentages at the final node level in a calculation node to be able to consume for analytics and insights.

 For example -

On the preview the result set to be

Observations:

  1. Alternatively calculate the percentages in the following layers or the Analytical Model
  2. This option could be considered too in scenarios of filtering with dimensions but of course the view or the table needs to be called thrice in the form of a graphical design.

To display percentage '%' for those metrics:

Refer to the following community blog posted- Display percentage for a calculated measure in Da... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Map Operation Activity to specific Recourse in SAP DMC
entry.content: Hi Manoel, Thanks for your input. using Resource Type, we are able to restrict the operation to start for mapped resources but in Work Center POD, it displays all the operations while using the operation / phase list plugin. Do we have any option to display only the allowed operations based on selected resources?

entry.title: Integration from SAP Sales Cloud V2 to S4HC Public Edition
entry.content:
(1) I would like to know that is it possible to configure the integration between SAP Sales Cloud V2 and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using standard APIs? If yes, could you please recommend me any API or reference link? I already searched in API Hub portal but can't find required API.

Required API lists from sales cloud v2 to s4hana public cloud:
1. Sales Quote Integration
2. Sales Order Integration
3. Opportunity to create Project in S4HANA
4. Price List Integration
5. Product Integration
6. Account Integration

(2) And which APIs are recommended to execute Sales Orders and Sales Quotes from SAP Sales Cloud V2?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Previewing Fiori Standard Extension Project on BAS
entry.content:
Do anyone know how can I preview an Extension Project on SAP Business Application Studio ?
I manage to preview other custom Fiori Apps, but cant preview any of the Extension Projects of Standard Fiori Projects, such as Look Up Retail Products.
Is there a version prerequisite or any steps that was missing ?

Many Thanks.

entry.title: can't start DB in DR after stop it
entry.content:
i start a DR on 2 hana database ( in 2  locations ) and now i think both working fine

but how i can use the secondary database if the primary data center get anything and i wanna to use the second one 

when i stop the DR and try to open it without DR i get error and can't use it .. and same when i try to take a backup from it i get a error in hana studio so how can i use the database in the 2nd location if i lose connection in the 1st one

entry.title: Re: MFA in IAS Admin Console
entry.content: Thanks Marko for the detailed explanation. Regarding first option: MFA via Risk Based Authentication Configuration, just to clarify you mean that the IAS administrators will enter the user and password but then they will be prompted with Two Factor Authentication (based on the method we setup) - please clarify my understanding. Regarding 2nd option: delegate authentication to corporate IDP, we have Azure as corporate IDP & have MFA enabled there, so we can setup under Conditional Authentication as Azure . Noted on the one admin creation as a backup in case trust fails. For avoiding passwords, could see the biometric options but X509 - can you please clarify. Also is there any standard recommendation or best practice for accessing IAS Admin Console. Thanks for your help.

Plaban

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: new partner id definition, alternative partner, bypass option

@Joberu this is a bug of the UI, this will be fixed soon, with the API you are able to overcome this as long as it hasn't be fixed.

Alex

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Variant Configuration SD Settings
entry.content: PP guy can handle this.

entry.title: Re: MDG Consolidation Beginners
entry.content: Hi, Is there any Step by Step Guide for MDG-M Consolidation as well

entry.title: Re: trying to upgrade to 2211.28, getting Cannot find CMS...
entry.content:
Hi,

I assume the provided url with localhost is what you expect so the error message clearly aims the root cause.

If current site is not stored in session (e.g. due to session timeout) the site is determined based on the url and if url doesn't match any pattern of any site the error is thrown. Make sure the url patterns are defined properly for your site and domain name you are using.

Please find more details in following KBA:

https://me.sap.com/notes/3081055/E

 

Regards,

MaciejPS

entry.title: Re: PO creation using BAPI_PO_CREATE1 when custom fields are in 'EKKO_INCL_EEW_PS'
entry.content: Question already asked and answered, please search: https://www.google.com/search?q=%22EKKO_INCL_EEW_PS%22%20site:sap.com (question "update of custom field added in EKKO in EKKO_INCL_EEW_PS...", just adapt the answer to your exact context)

entry.title: MDG Consolidation for Material- Process flow and Steps
entry.content:
Hello,

Can anyone help with Step by Step Process flow and guide on how to implement consolidation and mass processing for MDG-M. we have scenario to take input from multiple excel. How to consolidate them using CR and do mass processing. Kindly advise.

Thanks,

entry.title: Number range for trans./event type WL in year 2025 does not exist
entry.content:
Number range for trans./event type WL in year 2025 does not exist

During PGI in Delivery Page, This error message is poping up. How to resolve it.

entry.title: Re: PO creation using BAPI_PO_CREATE1 when custom fields...
entry.content: To update custom fields use the EXTENSIONIN parameter. Pass the structure EKKO with the custom field names and values in the VALUE field. Ensure the custom fields are mapped correctly to EKKO for the BAPI to process.

entry.title: CopyStep using Advanced Formula Step
entry.content:
Hello team,

We are trying to copy data from the Current version (months 1 to 6) to the Planning version, and data from months 7 to 12 from the Forecast version to the Planning version. However, due to the parameters requiring cardinality to be set to Single and level to Any, we cannot select multiple sourceDates.

I noticed that it might be possible to perform this CopyStep using an Advanced Copy Step, but we haven’t been able to achieve it. We tried the code in the attached screenshot but couldn’t get it to work.

Does anyone have any suggestions or advice?

Thank you!

entry.title: Change the description of AaKF in a planning area SAP IBP
entry.content:
Dear all,

is it possible to change the description of attribute as Key Figure in a planning area? In Attributes as Key Figure tab in PA I dont see Planning Area Attribute Description change option active.

I can't change the attribute description itself as it is standard one and is part of MD types of different areas. 

My interest is LEADTIMETRANSPORTATION attribute in Location Source MD.

 

Thank you!

BR, Halyna

#sapibp #SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain

Regards,
Ankit Kumar

Hemant

entry.title: Re: How to Call RAP Actions with Table Types from a List Report
entry.content:
@MioYasutake  - Did you try an example with raising error msgs in the static actions? I am calling a RFC - Destination NONE and looking at the best way to capture the error messages.

The deep parameter approach works perfectly.
I tried a similar apporach in my program in a mass create scenario.

entry.title: SAPUI5 Treetable client-side Binding without any ODATA SERVICE/Backend by using mockserver
entry.content:
Happy new year fellas!✨🎉
So I was playing around with the Treetable in SAPUI5, I had no backend-API/ODataservice to get the data from to bind my table with. Then i decided to go with the MockServer(Middleware)

 


We will navigate through the following steps :

STEP 1 : Creating a project in SAP BAS/VSCode IDE using metadata document - 

**NOTE - If you are using VSCode, you can directly enter the path of the metadata.xml ,and if you are using SAP BAS, you have to upload the metadata.xml file to your projects folder before going for project creation.(I did the same😅) ,sharing my metadata.xml if you in case you wanna use mine.

 

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" standalone="yes"?>
<edmx:Edmx Version="1.0"
xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2008/09/edm"
xmlns:edmx="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/06/edmx"
xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"
xmlns:edmanno="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2009/02/edm/annotation"
xmlns:sap="http://www.sap.com/Protocols/SAPData">

<edmx:DataServices m:DataServiceVersion="2.0">
    <Schema Namespace="DemoModel" xml:lang="en" sap:schema-version="1">
        
        <EntityType Name="Node">
            <Key>
                <PropertyRef Name="NodeID" />
            </Key>
            <Property Name="NodeID" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="false" edmanno:StoreGeneratedPattern="Identity" sap:hierarchy-node-for="NodeID"/>
            <Property Name="HierarchyLevel" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="false" sap:hierarchy-level-for="NodeID"/>
            <Property Name="Description" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="40" Unicode="true" FixedLength="false" />
            <Property Name="ParentNodeID" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="true" sap:hierarchy-parent-node-for="NodeID"/>
            <Property Name="DrillState" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="10" Unicode="true" FixedLength="false" sap:hierarchy-drill-state-for="NodeID" />
        </EntityType>
        
        <EntityContainer Name="DemoEntities" edmanno:LazyLoadingEnabled="true" m:IsDefaultEntityContainer="true">
            <EntitySet Name="Nodes" EntityType="DemoModel.Node" />
        </EntityContainer>
        
    </Schema>
</edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

 

 

STEP 2: Check the dependency that the mock server is installed.

And Also in ui5-mock.yaml if your mockdataPath is there is not.

**NOTE - If you need to install mock server manually to a project, see the following.

Install MockServer From Here 

STEP 3: Create your json file having your mock data in "webapp/localService/data/your_filename.json". my json(Nodes.json) is given below.

 

[
    {
        "NodeID": 1,
        "HierarchyLevel": 0,
        "Description": "Company",
        "ParentNodeID": null,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 2,
        "HierarchyLevel": 0,
        "Description": "Departments",
        "ParentNodeID": null,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 3,
        "HierarchyLevel": 0,
        "Description": "Projects",
        "ParentNodeID": null,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 4,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "HR",
        "ParentNodeID": 1,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 5,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Finance",
        "ParentNodeID": 1,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 6,
        "HierarchyLevel": 2,
        "Description": "Payroll",
        "ParentNodeID": 5,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 7,
        "HierarchyLevel": 2,
        "Description": "Budgeting",
        "ParentNodeID": 5,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 8,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "IT",
        "ParentNodeID": 2,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 9,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Marketing",
        "ParentNodeID": 2,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 10,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Sales",
        "ParentNodeID": 2,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 11,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Project A",
        "ParentNodeID": 3,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 12,
        "HierarchyLevel": 2,
        "Description": "Phase 1",
        "ParentNodeID": 11,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 13,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 1",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 14,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 2",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 15,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 3",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 16,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 4",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    }
]

 

STEP 4 : XML View with TreeTable Control. (If you are using Fiori Elements , the process is same upto here, and you should get the preview with hierachical data in table after step 3, just make sure your metadata is having the hierarchy oData annotations like my metadata, else it wont work😓).

 

<mvc:View controllerName="ztreetable.controller.View1"
    xmlns="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns:m="sap.m"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc">
    <m:Page id="page" >
        <TreeTable
            id="treeTable"
            selectionMode="Single"
            enableColumnReordering="false"
            rows="{
                path : '/Nodes',
                parameters : {
                    countMode: 'Inline',
                    numberOfExpandedLevels: 1
                }
            }">

            <columns>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="Description"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Link text="{Description}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="HierarchyLevel"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Text text="{HierarchyLevel}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="NodeID"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Text text="{NodeID}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="ParentNodeID"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Text text="{ParentNodeID}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

            </columns>

        </TreeTable>
    </m:Page>
</mvc:View>

 

STEP 5 : Preview your app using npm run start-mock 😎 (DONT USE NORMAL PREVIEW SCRIPT).
**NOTE - Make sure all your paths are correct and specially the name of the json file should be same as the pathname of "rows" attribute of TreeTable Control.

entry.title: Re: Unable to create Calculation Sheet - error "Business...
entry.content: Could you elaborate a bit more? How could there not be a rate when all the entries are maintained? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Out-of-the-box integration for credit card payments in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Prayash

as I understand from your diagram, your eCommerce system is directly connected with the Payment Service Provider (PSP). Therefore you obtain the "PSP Token". Before you send the Sales Order to the S/4HANA Public Cloud, the PSP Token must be replaced by by SAP Digital Payment token. Therefore you need to connect the eCommerce system with SAP digital payments to call the corresponding preparation service. This in in detail described in SAP help: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/DIGITALPAYMENTS/77d227ce58fd4629b483a6ed10d6dbf9/b66da73aee1b4e8fbd4762bb2316aefd.html

The data obtained from the mentioned services (e.g. DP Card Token, DP Authorization ID) are the input for the Sales Order API. The processing of the Sales Order / Billing / Financials will be performed by the S/4HANA system via the out-of-the-box integration. 

If you want to store the Card with the Business Partner for further usage (e.g. creation of manual Sales Orders with Credit Card), you can use an Business Partner API. Please follow the SAP Note  "3527605 - Creation of Payment Cards in Business Partner via API for SAP digital payment add-on". 

Kind regards, Martin

entry.title: Re: Email Verification Requirement for Member Login in SA...
entry.content:
Hi Kunal , 

Thanks for the response , but this is not exactly what i want 

if i enable email verification required then an email is been triggered , which i dont want , i want that email verification is required without sending an email out of CDC, so if a user is not verified it should return 206 and not 200 while login

entry.title: Re: Seamless Navigation in SAP Analytics Cloud: From Dashboards to Business Applications
entry.content:
Hello @Adem_Baykal,

Thanks for sharing the two possibilities! Does there exist a guide how an URL to a Fiori app in S4 has to be setup? We are searching for something similar than the guide to C4C deep links.

Many thanks in advance!

Best regards

Lothar

entry.title: Re: Number range for trans./event type WL in year 2025 do...
entry.content:
Hello @Subrata3096 

You need to define the missing number range(s) in OMBT. That should be part of your year end closure procedure.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP Data services 4.3 upgrade- RFC failure
entry.content:
We upgraded SAP data services from 4.2 to 4.3. We received the below RFC error, for the existing SAP connections

An error occurred while connecting to the server. :No RFC authorization for function module RFC_METADATA_GET.

We reached out SAP and understood that this is a known issue in 4.3 version and SAP recommended to implement the note # 3472602.  This is resulting in change of security authorizations in all the connected SAP systems.

Despite this RFC error, we are successfully able to Read data from RFC direct read and via ABAP dataflow and all the expected functionalities are working fine. How is it working despite RFC connection is at error ?. i get a feeling that we don't need to establish SAP connection in Management console at all. Please provide your thoughts.

entry.title: Re: Email Verification Requirement for Member Login in SA...
entry.content: Hi @Rvanjari, I don't think its possible, you can submit a SAP ticket to confirm with SAP. Kunal

entry.title: Re: Custom Extension in an MDK App (Using Metadata Approach)
entry.content: Are you creating a custom extension using metadata or building a native extension? How are you using the extension in the metadata? The error shown in the screenshot is trying to find the extension under the extensions/ folder, which is where all the native script plugin are based.

entry.title: Re: How to Edit and Annotate an Image in SAP MDK?
entry.content: SSAM team has already created this extension and is being used in the Service & Asset Manager app. You can try the demo version from the app store and check the functionality. Adding any new image or document will ask the user to annotate it. It's a native extension with platform-specific iOS (Swift) and Android (Java) code, so the source code will not be available.

entry.title: SAP S4/HANA Public Cloud- Create Custom Business Object and Custom Reusable Elements
entry.content:
Introduction

Hello, in this blog I will show you how to create custom business object (CBO) and custom reusable elements in S4/Hana public cloud. Then I will show you again what the code list I created in the custom reusable element does in the custom business object and why we created it.

Additional Information

Custom business objects help create business objects with SAP S/4Hana Cloud's user interface for extending data model fields, adding things like custom columns and calculation logic. With Custom Reusable Elements, you can customize the structure you create according to your needs and install it accordingly.

Custom Reusable Elements

Here I will create a house for sale ad example in the custom business object (CBO). The code list here will give us information about home types when we create an ad. First, let's create a custom code list from the custom reusable element.

Figure-1

You can follow the images below to create the creation phase. After these steps, we are done with custom reusable for now.

Figure-2

Figure-3

Custom Business Object

We move on to creating our example in the custom business object. Here again, you can follow the steps in the image.

Figure-4

Figure-5

It is necessary to select these boxes when creating a custom business object. These boxes have the following meanings in numerical order:

  • 1-Determination and Validation: It enables the business object (Create, edit, save) to have logic added. It is employed for both validation and the presentation of error messages. This can only be used when custom business object (CBO) is in publish status.
  • 2-User Interface: It makes an interface for maintenance. Comparable to SM30. The maintenance interface needs to be set to a custom catalog in order to be used.
  • 3-Back End Service: It generates an OData API that the SAP S/4HANA system can use. Communication Arrangements can be established following the creation of the API. This also applies to SAP/S4HANA Cloud, and we may use SAP BAS to develop a new Fiori application. CRUD operations are also within our capabilities. With SAP S/4HANA On-Premise, we may use SAP Cloud Connector to make the generated OData accessible.
  • 4-System Administrative Data: Additional fields are created when this option is checked. The creation date, creator, last modified date, and last modified are these extra fields. When a user creates a new business item, these fields are automatically filled up.

Figure-6

In the field section, you specify which fields you want to see in this house-ad table. In the picture, we use the house_type we created in custom reusable as the type of the house_type label. After selecting the type here, you can see the code list we created from the code list text on the right.Figure-7

In the Node section, when we run the last application, we will see that the house is a field.

Figure-8

You can also go to the fields section and select the node and add the fields of the node we added. After completing all the steps, you need to publish what you have done. After publishing, you can see what you have done in the log section.

Figure-9

Figure-10

After the publish phase, you need to perform operations numbered 1 and 2 from the general information section. These operations are;

  • 1. Maintain Catalogs: This is used to add the application to the catalog and create a box. When finished, it shows up in the launchpad and is allocated to the user.
  • 2. Go To Generated UI: This opens once it is fully published. brings up the user interface.

Figure-11

Selecting the Maintain catalog, you can select the catalog you want to add by clicking add on this screen and publish it. If you want, you can also create a custom catalog yourself, assign it to it and publish it. After publishing, when you click go to Generated UI, the application opens as an interface. Figure-12

Figure-13

Figure-14

You can open the application here by clicking Generated UI again. At the same time, you can go to the app finder, find the catalog you added the application to and add it to launchpad.

Figure-15

Figure-16

I created an example. Here you can see what the code list we created earlier and used in the fields does when creating an example.

Figure-17

Conclusion

As a result, today I showed you how to create a custom business object(CBO) and how to create a custom code list in a custom reusable element. Then I showed you again what the code list I created in a custom reusable element does in a custom business object and why we created it. I hope it will be useful for you.

entry.title: Re: Online Search feature not supported with download all...
entry.content: I dont believe there was ever an online search for technical objects in standard persona. You can check the current demo app and verify that. The online search is available for Material when adding parts and under Inventory clerk persona for certain use cases.

entry.title: Re: Exploring Accrual Engine By Examples Part 2 - Purchase Order Accruals
entry.content:
Hello Jason,

Thanks for your great blog. It's nicely written and detailed. One improvement though : I believe that a purchase order without GR would be a better actual example for accrual engine. For example, yearly maintenance costs for software ?

entry.title: Connecting Event Mesh from Cloud Foundry subaccount to CPI(Cloud Integration) in Neo subaccount
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

For our customer, we have SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) in Neo environment and SAP Event Mesh will be in Cloud Foundry Subaccount. Both are in different Global Account.

Could you please let us know whether we can be able to connect seamlessly between two environment (CF and Neo) to integrate SAP CPI and Event Mesh in customer landscape for  Event Driven Integration.

I would like to know the feasibility of this kind of setup and whether there will be any challenges or not?

Regards,

Souvik

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports and SQL encrypt connection using OLE...
entry.content: Great, as I explained once the connection is configurred in the Client CR will just work without having to configure anything else.

entry.title: RSRT returns the general exception RS_EXCEPTION 301 mentioning BLOCK_CHECK CL_RSR_VAR
entry.content:
After execution of query via RSRT in ABAP 7.40 SP23, error RS_EXCEPTION 301 (&1 >> Row: &4 Inc: &3 Prog: &2), inc: BLOCK_CHECK, Prog: CL_RSR_VAR:

How to get more details about this error? How to solve it?

entry.title: Re: Transaction ZAFN is not defined-VA11
entry.content: i have verified your opinion but still the error: This combination of item category ZSS and MRP type for material is not allowed.

entry.title: Re: RSRT returns the general exception RS_EXCEPTION 301 m...
entry.content:
By debug, find out which line in C_THX_VREP, here I found out that it's the line with VRNID = 0134:

Look at the values in the RANGE component of the:

I have debugged how the variable C_THX_VREP is filled, and I found out that it comes directly from the query definition, during the execution of the method CONSTRUCTOR of class CL_RSR_OLAP_VAR (inherits from CL_RSR_VAR), right after the call to the function module RRI_REPORT_IMPORT_AND_CHECK, whose contents is stored in the attribute O_SX_REPORT. Later, C_THX_VREP content is derived from O_SX_REPORT.

So, the issue is in the query definition.

Search in the query definition where "MVFI" is defined. In case of complex query, the most convenient way is to use the transaction code RSRTQ and then Print Preview. Find "MVFI" -> it's about the fund type:

Scroll up till you reach the top of the 4th vertical line -> Selection:

Continue till you reach the top of the 3rd, 2nd and 1st vertical lines:

So, the path is Query > Lines/Columns > Columns > Selection > Funds type MVFI.

In that case, the funds type MVFI did not exist for the financial area selected while running the BW query. The solution was to create it.

entry.title: Re: Prohibit copy, paste in SAP Advanced Workflow.
entry.content:
This is possible with an html field and some scripting. Here is an example:

a = form.getField('no_copy_paste')
a.setValue("""<html>
<html lang="en">
<body>
    <input type="text" id="no_copy_paste" placeholder="disablePaste">
    <script>
        document.getElementById('no_copy_paste').addEventListener('paste', function(event) {
            event.preventDefault();
        });
    </script>
</body>
</html>
""")
form.refreshElement('no_copy_paste')

This disables pasting on the client side, so if you have a determined user they could re-enable it using console commands.

entry.title: Unable to Use Account Assigmt "U" with Documt Type RV in BAPI_PR_CREATE
entry.content:
We encountered an error while attempting to create a mass creation tool for Purchase Requisition using BAPI_PR_CREATE. We found no issue with any other Document Type and Account Assignment combination except Document Type RV and Account Assignment U (Unknown). This combination works fine when using transaction ME51N but not when using BAPI_PR_CREATE. 
The issue arises during check-in Class Interface CL_ACCOUNT_MMSRV, precisely in line 127. I highlighted it in blue. Does anyone encounter such an error using BAPI_PR_CREATE to create PR for material or services? Your help on this is very much appreciated. 
CLASS: CL_ACCOUNT_MMSRV
METHOD: PROCESS
    IF  my_t_acc_line IS NOT INITIAL  OR
        my_t_service_data IS NOT INITIAL OR
         my_limit_data IS NOT INITIAL  OR my_ext_header-activity_type NE 'I'.

      IF my_acc_category_cust-kzvbr = 'U' AND
             ( my_t_acc_line IS NOT INITIAL OR my_t_acc_totals IS NOT INITIAL ).
        MESSAGE e005(srv) INTO lv_msg_dummy.
        CALL METHOD my_sp_header-message_ref->add_msg_to_bal
          EXPORTING
            im_struct_name = 'SRV_EXT_HEADER'
            im_field_name  = 'ACCTASSCAT'
            im_class_name  = 'CL_ACCOUNT_MMSRV'
            im_msgkey      = my_msg_key.
        RAISE error_account_process.
      ENDIF.
      CLEAR ls_acc_line.
      LOOP AT my_t_acc_line INTO ls_acc_line WHERE outline IS INITIAL AND
        srv_line IS NOT INITIAL .
        ls_acc_line-outline = 1.
        MODIFY my_t_acc_line FROM ls_acc_line.
      ENDLOOP.

entry.title: Adjustment of table xyz was aborted! Table xyz does not exist in the database
entry.content:
Hello, 

When I adjust table using quick fix on btp system I got an error and I couldn't adjust table. After that when I press the quick fix I can't see adjust table on list anymore. Just I can see continue adjustment of database table and this is not allowed, always get an error. 

Otherwise I can't delete same table because of error "xyz still being updated". How can I solve this problem?

entry.title: SAP ABAP-translation standard toolbar
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP Community 

When I open my session from the previous session,my standard toolbar remains partially in Turkish.

How can I fix this?

thanks,

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hi @Sanzenze,

Thank you for your question!

We have many customers successfully using the SAP Signavio tools. You can find some of their success stories here: Success Stories | SAP Signavio.

This platform is designed for customers to ask any questions they might have regarding our products. I encourage you to state your exact questions, and we will do our best to either answer them directly or connect you with someone who can.

Wishing you happy holidays!

entry.title: 如何变更S-账号的注册邮箱？How to update/change S-user email address?
entry.content:
请问如何修改我在SAP for Me 注册的S-账号的邮箱地址？


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 如何变更S-账号的注册邮箱？How to update/change S-user email address?
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Kindly know S-users can no longer manage their own email addresses in SAP for Me, only the administrators can. Also, administrators cannot change their own email addresses either, so another administrator must do this for them.

Product support engineer can not change email for S-user when there are active administrators.

So please kindly contact your company's Super Administrators, User Administrators, or Cloud Administrators (listed here) to change your S-user ID email address for them in the User Management application.

If the affected S-user is linked to SAP Universal ID, the user must first add the new email address to their UID in the SAP Universal ID Account Manager by following the steps in KBA 2882908.

Please refer to KBA 3108466 - How to update the email address for S-user IDs - SAP for Me.

Thanks and regards,
Joanna

entry.title: Not getting data in read entity for multiple entities
entry.content:
Here, i have a BO architecture as travel entity have a child as booking have a child as bookingsupplement.

I have 4 booking instances for travel id = 00000001. upon reading with first statement black boxed in ss (attached below) I can get 4 records in lt_result internal table. that's correct !!!

but when I try with the second statement red box, I am getting 1 record in lt_travel but 0 entries in lt_book internal table. My expectation is for a particular traveled eg. 000001, I should get all bookings and then for all those bookings in lt_book I should get booking supplements.  How should I proceed further and why it is behaving like this ?

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP-translation standard toolbar
entry.content: Okey, I find, Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP: Event on click of custom action with parameter
entry.content: if default function is not supported at backend, you can add the annotation manually at UI project side. so you can know when the action started.

entry.title: COPA segment and Strategic Bus Line is missing
entry.content:
Dear Friends,

In the COPA Document 1030556780, segment and Strategic Bus Line is missing, for rest of copa documents, all the fields in the copa document are displayed.

How and where to check , why  segment and Strategic Bus Line are missing. We are in ECC 6.0.

All the help will be greatly appreciated.

Regards
Sridhar

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: For Change CR Type - while changing any fiel...
entry.content:
Hi Sandeep,

Could you please elaborate, on what do you mean by any field is changed?

Please share a specific use case of what is the scenario. 

You can also use CL_USMD_MDF_CHANGE_DOCUMENT class to read change documents of an MDG CR.

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: MDG Consolidation for Material- Process flow and Steps
entry.content:
Hi Shweta,

Please refer to the consolidation and mass processing section of the community page How-to Information for SAP Master Data Governance | SAP Community to get basic details about consolidation process.

In consolidation or mass processing, at a time you can use only one excel sheet with multiple tabs ( eg: General data, Plant data ) to trigger your processes.

 

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: SAP Utilities Core - the Safe Way to Efficient and Innovative Cloud Solutions
entry.content: is there anything new planned coming year-s ?

entry.title: Replication is not happening in Mass processing upon adding Replication step at last step.
entry.content:
Replication is not happening in Direct Activation of Mass processing upon adding Replication step at last step after Activation step.

My process template has steps: Edit-> Validation-> Activation-> Replication

is there any work around to trigger replication upon completion of process template?

entry.title: Customer:Ctry-Spec.Enh. tab is missing in the Business Partner
entry.content:
Customer:Ctry-Spec.Enh. tab is missing in the Business Partner

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Customer:Ctry-Spec.Enh. tab is missing in the Busines...
entry.content: The Tab "Customer: Ctry-Spec. Enh." is Company code dependent data. Once a company code is been entered the new tab will appear. Maintain company code for related BP.

entry.title: Re: Update S4 Hana Public Cloud - Outbound Delivery Heade...
entry.content:
Hi Hanu,

Firstly, you are on the correct path, testing with Postman and prototyping the call for testing first. So Kudos for that.

If you check the Documents tab at the API Hub (https://api.sap.com/api/API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV_0002/documents), you will find the link to the Help.

The help does have a lot of valuable information and often has sample payloads 

In your case, its a little tricky based on the information you provided. Doesn't look like you are calling the correct operation for that API or you are missing data in the body.

The 401 Forbidden does usually signal a session or authentication problem, or an invalid CSRF token. (PS there are lots of blogs on that like here https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/csrf-token-handling-in-sap-api-management/ba-p/13500181). Or it could be that you are trying to change a field that is invalid (the ODATA Error app or one of the other Messaging apps in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition may provide some insight).

Since you are calling the header api, checking the help (see the section Operations for Outbound Delivery) (like https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/588780cab2774a7ab9fffca3a7f919fe/66a45c1c0ac04bed95d876d81a4ba6d3.html?locale=en-US) you can see in the Change Delivery Header section that the field Customer Shipping Instruction is not in the valid changeable fields for the header.

However, you may be to try the Change Outbound Delivery Header Text operation to do it - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/588780cab2774a7ab9fffca3a7f919fe/68279e9954ec4a40abe9bb3478daf9fa.html?locale=en-US

I would recommend to play around with the GET or check the api schema to check where that field is located in the Node.

I didn't dig too deep or look in the system, but I searched the help and the API schema and couldn't find the field "CustomerShippingInstruction" referenced in the API specifically, so it may be referenced by a text id or similar.

I hope this helps, if you could do some more testing and update us with more details, (like the get payload showing the field you want to update), someone may be able to provide more specific help.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Test Scripts for User Testing
entry.content: seems that the scripts need to be made by yourself & tailerozid depending on your SAP system settings

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP: Event on click of custom action with parameter
entry.content: Can you please elaborate on what annotation needs to be added and how to trigger ABAP code via the annotation?

entry.title: Re: Replication is not happening in Mass processing upon...
entry.content:
Hi Rishav,

Please check your output mode for your BO in DRF configuration, it has to be set to direct output to trigger automatic replication either after change request activation or after mass processing activation.

You can refer the below guide for reference.

Using Data Replication Framework for Material

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: I need to add a date dimension prompt to a universe
entry.content:
The error is actually a warning and it is correct, this object does not reference any tables.  However, it does not need to.  As written, it will not validate but it can still be used in a query as long as the query references a table.

You can validate the SQL by selecting any table but you won't want to leave a table selected or you'll have to create joins or it will cartesian.  So pick any table, validate it, then uncheck all before promoting the universe.

I also would recommend creating a naming standard for these type of objects so that when you run Integrity Check and see the warnings, you know you can ignore them.

entry.title: Re: Change in "Universal REST API integration" in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
If you display the data in your data variable, you will find it empty, which explains why you don't see anything further along. There is a fundamental problem, which is you are executing a list, but you are returning an object (see your first screenshot).

There are 2 ways to do this:

  1. Fix the current way.
  2. Probably use the right data source.

To fix the current project using a REST data resource, you need to return a list in your list action. SO you have o get rid of everything else besides your list in the response. To do this, use the response mapper. It already has the basic coding, so you just add "value" to be the top level to be returned in the response.

{records: response.data.value, driverCode: response.status}

 

Now you use he data variable for the repeat, and the bind the fields to "data items in repeat". Not sure what you are doing with the nesting repeats, since there are no nested objects in the response.

But all his begs the question: Why not use the OData data resource for this OData API?

In the past people wanted to send complex OData filters, and this was not possible with the OData data resource, but now you can define actions, which will let you do all that, so I would think that is the way o go.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: For Change CR Type - while changing any fiel...
entry.content: Thanks for the replay Anirban, @0012anirban

entry.title: Re: I need to add a date dimension prompt to a universe
entry.content: Sorry for duplicate answer, I didn't see that my answer yesterday posted.

entry.title: Re: Difference between End User and Key User
entry.content:
Hello

End user = ordinary user, and Key user = it's a key user ^^.

For example, the latter may be the person chosen to describe the needs of his team/service -  or the person who shall be trained first in the new software application, and then after he will train the ordinary users in his team.

The key is the difference between them, because he has more responsability on his team and he is not necessary an admin, just a referent if you prefer

KR

Adile

entry.title: How to add a filter to an "overview page" fiori application ?
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

My customer, running a SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition system, would like to add a filter to fiori application "Quality Engineer Overview" (F2360, which is a "SAP Fiori elements: Overview Page" kind of fiori application). The new filter to add should correspond to the Material's group:

Would this be possible please?

I tried checking but: 

- not possible to do anything with the UI adaptation mode other than changing the cards themselves (hide some or change of their attributes)

- SAP Fiori library says that Fiori app F2360 can be modified with "sap ui5 adaptation project" but would this fulfill my requirement?

Thank you all for your help

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: For Change CR Type - while changing any fiel...
entry.content:
Thanks for the replay Anirban, CL_USMD_MDF_CHANGE_DOCUMENT it will be happening after submission of CR. But Actual scenario required is once USER entered into change CR Type, if changing any field error message should get display.

@0012anirban

entry.title: Re: Include Logo on Request Form in SBPA
entry.content:
Do you mean forms that appear in the inbox (notifications and approvals)?

You can now create UI5 forms instead of using the built in editor, in which case you make anything you want. There is no feature like in Work Zone where you can sub in your own logo to appear in the corner.

I haven't seen any other backlog item about this.

entry.title: Re: Workaround Needed for Free-hand SQL with DDL statemen...
entry.content: If you are trying to use temp tables, create a stored procedure that uses a temp table then use an ODBC connection for Webi FHSQL.
